tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

Interesting to note here, the initial surge in the beginning of the video are all Iran-linked tankers (on the sanction list).

In the tail-end of the video, there are two ships related to Korea.

HMM DAON (IMO: 9869227): Container Ship

UNIVERSAL GLORY (IMO: 9794824): VLCC

The rest of the ships (at the end) are related to Iran.

Based on today's movement, it seems that IRGC is still throttling flows but not closing the Strait entirely.

Source: @MarineTraffic
tweets.full_text:

Based on today's movement, it seems that IRGC is still throttling flows but not closing the Strait entirely.

The only place for true Korean equity price discovery is on Hyperliquid. Korean consistently sidecars, and bands short selling//circuit breakers their equities.

Hyperliquid is the cheapest place in the world to place a sports bet on their outcome markets.

tweets.text:
Taking a break from the charts for a minute to look at the tech, which I think is what will make the real difference when we start to rally again.

Sui launched Seal MPC on mainnet a few days ago.

It’s smart locks for your data and AI stuff where the unlock key gets split across a group of operators so no single one holds the full key.

Same simple code for builders with stronger security. Makes it safer for AI agents that need to touch data or money without full access.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Think the stack SUI has been focusing on the past several months with free txs for payments, private txs, enhanced security, etc. should set them up for a solid run when the broader market finds a bottom.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Seal MPC is live on Mainnet.

Seal is the programmable access layer for data, AI, & digital assets.

Builders can now choose between independent key servers, MPC committees, or a combination of both, bringing stronger trust assumptions to encrypted data, AI, & digital assets. https://t.co/hRuFKQaHi2
tweets.full_text:
Taking a break from the charts for a minute to look at the tech, which I think is what will make the real difference when we start to rally again.

Sui launched Seal MPC on mainnet a few days ago.

It’s smart locks for your data and AI stuff where the unlock key gets split across a group of operators so no single one holds the full key.

Same simple code for builders with stronger security. Makes it safer for AI agents that need to touch data or money without full access.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Think the stack SUI has been focusing on the past several months with free txs for payments, private txs, enhanced security, etc. should set them up for a solid run when the broader market finds a bottom.
